mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises discount rates applied to equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high inflation readings and elevated federal funds rates compress real disposable income while raising borrowing costs, jointly driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint inflation-rate signal fails to predict negative returns when both series are elevated, or forward returns show no significant difference between high-inflation/high-rate regimes and baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production indicates supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing output, which suppresses corporate earnings expectations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production contractions do not correlate with negative equity returns, or the signal produces positive forward returns in more than 50% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Stronger quarterly GDP growth expands corporate revenue bases and improves earnings forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth prints fail to predict positive equity returns, or the Rank IC drops below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index breaches 285, persistent inflation expectations force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, depressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds 285, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Concurrent payroll growth and unemployment declines do not drive positive short-term returns, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in forward return regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding retail sales indicate robust consumer demand and business investment, which lift equity valuations and produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rising housing starts and retail sales fail to predict positive equity returns, or the signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.
